words.word: A stitch in time saves nine
words.definition: Prompt action prevents bigger problems later.
words.example: Fix that leak now — a stitch in time saves nine.

words.word: Actions speak louder than words
words.definition: What people do reveals more than what they say.
words.example: Actions speak louder than words; watch him.

words.word: A picture is worth a thousand words
words.definition: Images convey information more effectively than words.
words.example: Show them the graph — a picture is worth a thousand words.

words.word: Better late than never
words.definition: Doing something late is better than not doing it at all.
words.example: He apologised — better late than never.

words.word: Birds of a feather flock together
words.definition: People of similar character tend to associate.
words.example: They all love chess; birds of a feather flock together.

words.word: Don't judge a book by its cover
words.definition: Do not evaluate something only by its outward appearance.
words.example: She seems shy, but don't judge a book by its cover.

words.word: Don't count your chickens before they hatch
words.definition: Do not assume success before it happens.
words.example: We might win, but don't count your chickens before they hatch.

words.word: Every dog has its day
words.definition: Everyone gets a chance eventually.
words.example: Keep trying — every dog has its day.

words.word: Fortune favours the bold
words.definition: Brave action often leads to success.
words.example: She applied for the top job; fortune favours the bold.

words.word: Haste makes waste
words.definition: Rushing leads to mistakes and lost effort.
words.example: Slow down — haste makes waste.

words.word: Honesty is the best policy
words.definition: Being truthful is always the wisest course.
words.example: Just admit it — honesty is the best policy.

words.word: If it ain't broke, don't fix it
words.definition: Do not change what is working well.
words.example: Leave the system alone; if it ain't broke, don't fix it.

words.word: Look before you leap
words.definition: Consider possible consequences before acting.
words.example: Sign nothing yet — look before you leap.

words.word: Necessity is the mother of invention
words.definition: Difficulty inspires creative solutions.
words.example: He built a cooler from a bucket; necessity is the mother of invention.

words.word: No man is an island
words.definition: No one is completely independent of others.
words.example: Ask for help — no man is an island.

words.word: Practice makes perfect
words.definition: Regular practice leads to mastery.
words.example: Keep drilling those scales — practice makes perfect.

words.word: Rome wasn't built in a day
words.definition: Important work takes time.
words.example: Be patient with the project; Rome wasn't built in a day.

words.word: The early bird catches the worm
words.definition: Those who act promptly get the best opportunities.
words.example: She got there first — the early bird catches the worm.

words.word: The grass is always greener on the other side
words.definition: People often envy others' situations.
words.example: He regrets moving; the grass is always greener on the other side.

words.word: The pen is mightier than the sword
words.definition: Writing and ideas are more powerful than force.
words.example: Peaceful protest works — the pen is mightier than the sword.

words.word: There's no place like home
words.definition: Home is the best and most comforting place.
words.example: After the trip she sighed, there's no place like home.

words.word: Two heads are better than one
words.definition: Two people working together achieve more.
words.example: Let's plan it together — two heads are better than one.

words.word: Where there's a will, there's a way
words.definition: Determination overcomes obstacles.
words.example: She learned to walk again; where there's a will, there's a way.

words.word: You reap what you sow
words.definition: Your actions bring corresponding results.
words.example: He treated people badly and now suffers — you reap what you sow.

words.word: A watched pot never boils
words.definition: Time seems to pass slowly when one is anxiously waiting.
words.example: Stop staring at the phone; a watched pot never boils.

words.word: Absence makes the heart grow fonder
words.definition: Being apart strengthens affection.
words.example: She missed him terribly — absence makes the heart grow fonder.

words.word: All good things must come to an end
words.definition: Nothing pleasant lasts forever.
words.example: The holiday is over; all good things must come to an end.

words.word: All that glitters is not gold
words.definition: Attractive things are not always valuable.
words.example: Read the fine print — all that glitters is not gold.

words.word: An apple a day keeps the doctor away
words.definition: Eating fruit daily keeps you healthy.
words.example: She snacks on apples — an apple a day keeps the doctor away.

words.word: Beauty is in the eye of the beholder
words.definition: Perceptions of beauty are subjective.
words.example: He liked the odd sculpture; beauty is in the eye of the beholder.

words.word: Beggars can't be choosers
words.definition: Those in need must accept what is offered.
words.example: Take the free room — beggars can't be choosers.

words.word: Blood is thicker than water
words.definition: Family bonds are stronger than other ties.
words.example: She defended her brother; blood is thicker than water.

words.word: Charity begins at home
words.definition: One's first duty is to one's own family.
words.example: Help your parents first — charity begins at home.

words.word: Curiosity killed the cat
words.definition: Being too inquisitive can be dangerous.
words.example: Don't peek in there — curiosity killed the cat.

words.word: Do unto others as you would have them do unto you
words.definition: Treat others as you wish to be treated.
words.example: Be kind — do unto others as you would have them do unto you.

words.word: Don't bite the hand that feeds you
words.definition: Do not harm those who help you.
words.example: Respect your boss — don't bite the hand that feeds you.

words.word: Don't put off till tomorrow what you can do today
words.definition: Do not procrastinate.
words.example: Finish the essay tonight — don't put off till tomorrow what you can do today.

words.word: Easy come, easy go
words.definition: Things gained without effort are lost as easily.
words.example: He won the money and spent it — easy come, easy go.

words.word: Empty vessels make the most noise
words.definition: Foolish people talk the loudest.
words.example: Ignore his boasting — empty vessels make the most noise.

words.word: Every rose has its thorn
words.definition: Every good thing has some drawback.
words.example: The job pays well but is stressful — every rose has its thorn.

words.word: Familiarity breeds contempt
words.definition: Knowing someone too well can reduce respect.
words.example: They quarrel constantly — familiarity breeds contempt.

words.word: First come, first served
words.definition: Those who arrive first are served first.
words.example: Tickets go on sale at nine — first come, first served.

words.word: God helps those who help themselves
words.definition: People must make their own efforts.
words.example: Apply for jobs; God helps those who help themselves.

words.word: Good things come to those who wait
words.definition: Patience is rewarded.
words.example: Keep saving — good things come to those who wait.

words.word: Great minds think alike
words.definition: Clever people often reach the same conclusions.
words.example: We both suggested it; great minds think alike.

words.word: He who laughs last laughs longest
words.definition: Ultimate success is what matters.
words.example: She lost round one but won overall — he who laughs last laughs longest.

words.word: Home is where the heart is
words.definition: Home is wherever one's loved ones are.
words.example: She moved to be with him; home is where the heart is.

words.word: If you can't beat them, join them
words.definition: If you can't defeat opponents, become an ally.
words.example: Everyone runs marathons now — if you can't beat them, join them.

words.word: It's better to be safe than sorry
words.definition: Precaution is preferable to regret.
words.example: Wear a helmet — better safe than sorry.

words.word: Laughter is the best medicine
words.definition: A cheerful attitude aids recovery and wellbeing.
words.example: Watch comedies; laughter is the best medicine.

words.word: Let bygones be bygones
words.definition: Forgive past offences and move on.
words.example: We agreed to let bygones be bygones.

words.word: Many hands make light work
words.definition: A task is easier when many help.
words.example: Everyone pitch in — many hands make light work.

words.word: Money doesn't grow on trees
words.definition: Money is not obtained without effort.
words.example: Stop wasting cash — money doesn't grow on trees.

words.word: Never look a gift horse in the mouth
words.definition: Do not criticise something given for free.
words.example: Take the phone; never look a gift horse in the mouth.

words.word: Nothing ventured, nothing gained
words.definition: Without risk there is no reward.
words.example: Just ask her out — nothing ventured, nothing gained.

words.word: One good turn deserves another
words.definition: A kindness should be returned.
words.example: Let me help you — one good turn deserves another.

words.word: Out of sight, out of mind
words.definition: People and things forgotten when not seen.
words.example: Since he left the city, out of sight, out of mind.

words.word: People who live in glass houses shouldn't throw stones
words.definition: Do not criticise faults you have yourself.
words.example: She should not judge; people in glass houses shouldn't throw stones.

words.word: Silence is golden
words.definition: Sometimes it is best to say nothing.
words.example: In this argument, silence is golden.

words.word: Slow and steady wins the race
words.definition: Consistent effort achieves success.
words.example: Study daily; slow and steady wins the race.

words.word: Still waters run deep
words.definition: Quiet people often have depth of character.
words.example: He hardly speaks, but still waters run deep.

words.word: Strike while the iron is hot
words.definition: Act at the best opportunity.
words.example: Send the offer now — strike while the iron is hot.

words.word: The apple doesn't fall far from the tree
words.definition: Children resemble their parents.
words.example: She's stubborn like her mother; the apple doesn't fall far from the tree.

words.word: The best things in life are free
words.definition: The most valuable things cost nothing.
words.example: Time with family — the best things in life are free.

words.word: The customer is always right
words.definition: Businesses should defer to customers' opinions.
words.example: Refund it; the customer is always right.

words.word: The road to hell is paved with good intentions
words.definition: Good intentions do not excuse harmful outcomes.
words.example: He meant well but caused chaos — the road to hell is paved with good intentions.

words.word: The squeaky wheel gets the grease
words.definition: Those who complain loudest get attention.
words.example: Keep asking — the squeaky wheel gets the grease.

words.word: The truth will out
words.definition: Truth cannot stay hidden forever.
words.example: They'll uncover the fraud — the truth will out.

words.word: Time is money
words.definition: Time wasted is money lost.
words.example: Hurry up — time is money.

words.word: Time heals all wounds
words.definition: Emotional pain fades with time.
words.example: Give it a year — time heals all wounds.

words.word: Too many cooks spoil the broth
words.definition: Too many people involved ruin a task.
words.example: Only two of us should plan it — too many cooks spoil the broth.

words.word: Truth is stranger than fiction
words.definition: Real life is often more unusual than made-up stories.
words.example: No one would believe it — truth is stranger than fiction.

words.word: Variety is the spice of life
words.definition: New and varied experiences make life interesting.
words.example: Try a new hobby; variety is the spice of life.

words.word: When in Rome, do as the Romans do
words.definition: Adopt local customs when you visit a place.
words.example: Bow when greeted — when in Rome, do as the Romans do.

words.word: When the cat's away, the mice will play
words.definition: People misbehave when supervision is absent.
words.example: With their teacher out, when the cat's away the mice will play.

words.word: You can lead a horse to water but you can't make it drink
words.definition: You can give someone a chance, not force them to use it.
words.example: I offered advice — you can lead a horse to water but you can't make it drink.

words.word: You can't have your cake and eat it too
words.definition: You cannot enjoy both of two mutually exclusive options.
words.example: Choose one — you can't have your cake and eat it too.

words.word: You can't teach an old dog new tricks
words.definition: It is hard to change long-established habits.
words.example: He won't learn the software — you can't teach an old dog new tricks.

words.word: A friend in need is a friend indeed
words.definition: A true friend helps in times of trouble.
words.example: She came at once — a friend in need is a friend indeed.

words.word: A chain is only as strong as its weakest link
words.definition: A group is limited by its weakest member.
words.example: Train the new intern — a chain is only as strong as its weakest link.

words.word: A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step
words.definition: Great tasks start with small first actions.
words.example: Just write one page — a journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.

words.word: A leopard cannot change its spots
words.definition: People rarely change their nature.
words.example: He'll lie again; a leopard cannot change its spots.

words.word: An ounce of prevention is worth a pound of cure
words.definition: Preventing problems is easier than fixing them.
words.example: Get the vaccine — an ounce of prevention is worth a pound of cure.

words.word: As you make your bed, so you must lie in it
words.definition: You must accept the results of your choices.
words.example: You chose this path; as you make your bed, so you must lie in it.

words.word: Beauty is only skin deep
words.definition: Character matters more than looks.
words.example: Kindness lasts — beauty is only skin deep.

words.word: Better safe than sorry
words.definition: It is wiser to be cautious.
words.example: Wear the seatbelt — better safe than sorry.

words.word: Cleanliness is next to godliness
words.definition: Being clean is virtuous.
words.example: Tidy your room — cleanliness is next to godliness.

words.word: Do not bite off more than you can chew
words.definition: Do not attempt more than you can manage.
words.example: Drop a course — don't bite off more than you can chew.

words.word: Don't cross the bridge until you come to it
words.definition: Do not worry about a problem until it arises.
words.example: We'll see about that; don't cross the bridge until you come to it.

words.word: Every man has his price
words.definition: Anyone can be bribed for enough money.
words.example: They found his weakness; every man has his price.

words.word: Give a man enough rope and he will hang himself
words.definition: Given freedom, a fool will ruin himself.
words.example: Let him talk; give a man enough rope and he will hang himself.

words.word: Give credit where credit is due
words.definition: Acknowledge people's contributions.
words.example: Thank the intern — give credit where credit is due.

words.word: God helps those who help themselves
words.definition: People must act to solve their own problems.
words.example: Send the applications; God helps those who help themselves.

words.word: Half a loaf is better than none
words.definition: A little of something is better than nothing.
words.example: Take the offer — half a loaf is better than none.

words.word: Hindsight is twenty-twenty
words.definition: Past events are easy to understand after they happen.
words.example: We should have sold; hindsight is twenty-twenty.

words.word: Ignorance is bliss
words.definition: Not knowing something unpleasant can be comforting.
words.example: She doesn't know he lied — ignorance is bliss.

words.word: It never rains but it pours
words.definition: Troubles come all at once.
words.example: Car broke, roof leaked — it never rains but it pours.

words.word: Jack of all trades, master of none
words.definition: Someone skilled at many things but expert at none.
words.example: He tries everything — jack of all trades, master of none.

words.word: Let the punishment fit the crime
words.definition: Penalties should match the offence.
words.example: A warning is enough; let the punishment fit the crime.

words.word: Little strokes fell great oaks
words.definition: Small efforts accomplish big things over time.
words.example: Save a little each week — little strokes fell great oaks.

quiz.question: Which word means: "Prompt action prevents bigger problems later."?
options: A stitch in time saves nine
A friend in need is a friend indeed
Many hands make light work
Don't cross the bridge until you come to it
quiz.explanation: A stitch in time saves nine (proverb) — Prompt action prevents bigger problems later.

quiz.question: Which word means: "What people do reveals more than what they say."?
options: Honesty is the best policy
One good turn deserves another
Charity begins at home
Actions speak louder than words
quiz.explanation: Actions speak louder than words (proverb) — What people do reveals more than what they say.

quiz.question: Which word means: "Images convey information more effectively than words."?
options: A picture is worth a thousand words
Necessity is the mother of invention
Time is money
Half a loaf is better than none
quiz.explanation: A picture is worth a thousand words (proverb) — Images convey information more effectively than words.

quiz.question: Which word means: "Doing something late is better than not doing it at all."?
options: Necessity is the mother of invention
Laughter is the best medicine

quiz.explanation: Better late than never (proverb) — Doing something late is better than not doing it at all.

options: Let the punishment fit the crime
